3.why: We need sizes to wire the chain and the dummy.
3.invariant: sz is the number of real nodes.

4.why: nextp[i] holds the id node i points to.

5.happening: Loop over every real node to wire the chain.
5.why: Each node links to the next.

6.happening: Node {i} is not the tail — point it forward.
6.why: Interior nodes link to the next.
6.invariant: Only the tail ends the list.

6.why: The last node points at nothing.

10.why: It parallels the nodes and adds the dummy value.
10.invariant: disp_vals[i] is node i's shown value.

11.happening: Copy each real node's value for display.

13.why: The dummy is drawn before the head.
13.invariant: The last display slot is the dummy.

14.happening: The dummy's id ({dummy}) sits just past the real nodes.
14.why: A dummy before the head simplifies removing the first node.
14.invariant: dummy is a stable sentinel id.

15.happening: Point the dummy at the head (node 0).
15.why: The dummy leads into the real list.

16.why: It flashes the link we rewire.
16.invariant: changed holds the last rewired node id.

17.why: slow will end just before the target.
17.invariant: slow trails fast by the gap.

18.happening: Start fast on the dummy too.
18.invariant: Both begin together on the dummy.

19.happening: Track how many nodes fast has advanced.
19.why: We open a gap of exactly n.

20.happening: Open the gap — advance fast (advanced {moved} of {n}).
20.why: A fixed n-node gap lets slow stop just before the target.
20.invariant: fast leads slow by moved nodes.

20.why: fast is now n nodes ahead of slow.
20.invariant: fast leads slow by exactly n.

23.happening: fast isn't at the last node yet — slide both forward.
23.why: Keeping the gap fixed until fast hits the end lands slow before the target.
23.invariant: fast stays n ahead of slow.

23.happening: fast is at the last node — slow sits just before the target.
23.why: The gap of n means slow.next is the nth from the end.
23.invariant: slow.next is the node to remove.

25.invariant: fast stays n ahead of slow.

26.happening: removed = the node right after slow — the one to delete.
26.why: That's the nth node from the end.

27.happening: Unlink it: slow's next skips over the removed node.
27.why: Bypassing the node removes it from the list.
27.invariant: slow now points past the removed node.

29.happening: The new head is whatever the dummy points to.
29.why: If the head itself was removed, this reflects it.
29.invariant: head is the first remaining node (or -1).

30.happening: Start an empty list to read out the result.
30.invariant: order will hold the remaining values.

31.happening: Begin the read-out at the head.

32.invariant: order holds the values read so far.

32.happening: Reached the end — the result is complete.

34.why: Move to the next remaining node.

35.why: The nth-from-end node has been removed in one pass.

init.happening: Building the chain, the dummy sentinel, and placing slow and fast on the dummy.
init.why: We need the list and both pointers before opening the gap.
init.invariant: slow and fast start together on the dummy.

loop.happening: Opening the n-gap, sliding both pointers, or reading out.
loop.why: One pass: gap then slide then read.
loop.invariant: fast leads slow by n during the slide.

move.happening: Advancing slow, fast, or the read-out cursor.
move.invariant: The gap between slow and fast stays n.

update.happening: Counting, or unlinking the target node.
update.why: The removal is a single next-pointer rewrite.
